course_title: Clinical Exercise Science for Sports Medicine 1
course_description:
Develops theoretical and practical skills focussing on the cardiovascular system during exercise. Topics covered include physiological responses of the cardiovascular system in response to exercise, exercise ECG interpretations and the role of exercise in the primary, secondary and tertiary prevention of cardiovascular disease.

SPMD7005 - Clinical Exercise Science for Sports Medicine 1.

SPMD7005 is a core course in the Masters of Sports Medicine , Australia's longest running post-graduate program , and covers materials across learning areas identified by an international Delphi consensus for Sports & Exercise Medicine programs (Humphries et al. BJSM . 2021; 55:81-83). See online supplemental document for specifics:

https://bjsm.bmj.com/content/bjsports/55/2/81/DC1/embed/inline-supplementary-material-1.pdf?download=true

SPMD7005 focuses on key principles in "Sports & Exercise Science" (e.g., exercise physiology, sports nutrition, sports and exercise psychology, physical activity promotion etc) within the multidisciplinary field of sports medicine.ﾠA broad range of topics , with an emphasis on evidence-based and clinically important aspects, are covered in the course syllabus for SPMD7005 as per the comprehensive list of online lectures and materials below:

Physiology - Training Principles in Sports & Exercise Medicine, Over-reaching & Over-training in Athletes, Endurance Training for Athetes - Cardiovascular Adaptations, Endurance Training in Athletes - Skeletal Muscle Adaptations, Energy Systems & Exercise, Muscle Fibre Types & Exercise, Ergogenic (Creatine, Caffeine, Sodium Bicarbonate, Beta Analine, Beetroot etc), ﾠResistance Training Principle, Resistance Training – Children, Resistance Training - Older Individuals, Resistance Training - Eccentric Exercise, High Intensity Interval Training (HIIT), Fluids & Hydration in Exercise. Physical Activity & Sport Psychology - Exercise & Mental Health, Exercise & Depression, Exercise & Cancer, Sport Psychology – Overview, Sport Psychology – Motivation, Sport Psychology – Behaviour, Sport Psychology – Needs, Sport Psychology – Focus, Sports Performance & Anxiety

SPMD7005 assumes no background except for the entry requirements to the program. As with all courses within the Sports Medicine program, all materials within SPMD7005 will be accessed in online format only and involves all off-campus (external) learning . A comprehensive syllabus of online lectures, case based learning and related materials has been developed to facilitate the learning and integration of materials in SPMD7005.

Updated and new materials will be added to from time to time in line with topical and emerging topics relevant to sports and exercise medicine. We will email you during the semester when new or updated materials are uploaded for your course.

Reading of textbook chapters and course papers is an essential requirement for SPMD7005.ﾠ

course_aims:
The overall aim of SPMD7005 is to promote the development of knowledge on the underlying principles and practices of "Sports and Exercise Science".ﾠ

Through the course and assessment materials in SPMD7005 we aim to create opportunities for you to:

Achievement of these aims will be facilitated through the following learning and assessment materials:

learning_outcomes.description: demonstrate a sound understanding of sports and exercise science in the field of sports medicine
demonstrate a sound understanding of common practices / roles of nutrition in sports and exercise
demonstrate a sound understanding of ergogenic aids in sports and exercise
demonstrate a sound understanding of basic principles in sports psychology
demonstrate a sound understanding of physical activity (inactivity) / exercise effects / prescription for patients
demonstrate well developed clinical knowledge, reasoning and communication skills for successful completion of case study videos on specific topics in Sports & Exercise Medicine

assessment_details.title: Online MCQ Exam 1
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: (Pre-Census) Online Multiple Choice Question Exam to assess learning of materials covered in web video lectures & appropriate textbook chapters for SPMD7005. Materials from Weeks 1 to 4 (inclusive) will be covered. The MCQ exams are structured within an overall framework of 2 minutes per question - for example a 50 minute time period would be set for a 25 question MCQ quiz. Each question has only one (1) correct answer and no marks are deducted for incorrect answers. Read full task description Hide full task description

learning_activities.topic: Video Lectures Online Video Lectures (Lecture Series): SPMD7005 involves self-directed learning from a comprehensive series of expert online lectures and materials associated readings and textbook chapters (Brukner & Khan). The lectures and associated course materials which are organized in fortnightly "blocks" are progressively sequenced from Weeks 1-2 to Week 13 . The lecture series have been planned and developed by the joint course co-coordinators - Dr Anita Green, Dr Peter Friis and AProf Craig Engstrom. SPMD7005 is based around a core set of lectures from the joint course coordinators and expert contributors such as AProf David Jenkins, Prof Cliff Mallet, etc. SPMD7005 covers a broad spectrum of topics central to sports and exercise medicine. Readings/Ref: Blackboard URL ; Clinical Sports Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
